The Islamic Republic of Pakistan
And .
The Federal Republic of Grmany
Hereinafter referred to individually as "Contractirg State" and collectively as
"Contracting States",
Desiring to intensify economic co-operation between both States,
Intending to create favorable conditions for investments Dy investors of either State in the
territory of the other State,
Recognizing that the encouragement and protection oi such investments can stimulate
private business initiative and increase the prosperity of )oth Contracting States,
Have agreed as follows:
Article 1
Definitions
For the purposes of this Agreement
(cid:9)
(1) the term "investments" comprises evey kind of asset, established or
acquired by an investor of one Contricting State in the territory of the
other contracting State in accordance with the laws and regulations of
the latter Coniracting State, in particular:
movable and immovable properti, as well as other rights in rem,
such as mortgages, liens and pled es;
shares of companies and other kir ds of interest in companies;
claims to money or to any other Derformance having an economic
value associated with an investnunt;

intellectual property rights, in particu at copyrights, patents, utility model
patents, industrial designs, trade ma
.ks, trade names, trade and business
secrets, technical processes, know boy, and good will;
business concessions under public lay, including concessions to search
for, extract and exploit natural resources;
Any alteration of the form in which as.sets
ire invested shall not affect their
classification as investment;
Mere construction and service contracts that do nyt include an investment componc:nt
do not all under the definition of investment uncle. this Agreement;
(2) the term "investor" means
in respect of the Federal Republic of Gernany:
- Germans within the meaning of the Basic Lav of the Federal Republic of
Germany,
any juridical person as well as any commercial or other company
cir
association with or without legal personality having its seat in the territory of
the Federal Republic of Germany, irrespective of whether or not its activities
are directed at profit,
in respect of the Islamic Republic of Paki:tan:
- Pakistanis within the
meaning of the laws of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan,

- any juridical person or any company or association, incorporated in the
territory of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan and lawfully existing in
accordance with its legislation;
the term "company or companies" of a Cmtracting State means corporations,
firms and associations incorporated or constituted or established under the law in force
in the territory of a Contracting State whether privaely or state owned;
without prejudice to any other method of detemining nationality, in particular any
person in possession of a national passport issuxi by the competent authorities of the
Contracting' State concerned shall be deemed to be a national of that Contracting
State;
the term "returns" means the amount yielded by an investment for a definite
period, such as profit, dividends, interest, royalties o. fees;
the term "territory" means the land and territorial sea as well as the exclusive
economic zone and the continental shelf where a Contracting State exercises sovereign
rights or jurisdiCtion in accordance with the pro-isions of international law and its
domestic law.
Article 2
Admission, Promotion and Protection of Investments
Each Contracting Stale shall in its territory piomote as far as possible investments
by investors of the other Contracting State and adnit such investments in accordance
with its legislation.
(2)(cid:9)
Each Contracting State shall in its territory in any case accord investments by
investors of the other Contacting State, fair and equitable treatment as well as fall
protection and security in accordance with customar3 international law.

Returns from the investment and, in the event of their re-investment, the returns
therefrom shall enjoy the same protection as the investment.
Neither Contracting State shall in any way impair by arbitrary
or discriminatory
measures the management, maintenance, use, erjoyment, sale or other disposal of
investments in its territory of investors of the other Contracting State.
Each Contracting State shall, in its terziory, respect and protect intellecr.ual
property rights, aS defined and referred to in the Agreement on Trade Related Aspects of
Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS), of the invest)rs of the other Contracting State, in
accordance with its national legislation, rules and regulations.
Article 3
National Treatment and Most-Favo-ed-Nation
Treatment
Each Contracting State shall accord to inveKors of the other Contracting State and
their investments owned or controlled by them treatment no less favorable than that it
accords to its own investors and their investments cr to investors and their investments of
any third State.
The following shall, in particular, be deemed "treatment less favorable": unequal
treatment in the case of restrictions on the purclase of raw or auxiliary materials, of
energy or fuel or of means of production or opera ion of any kind, unequal treatmeat in
the case of impeding the marketing of products imide or outside the country, as well as
any other measure having similar effects. Measurs that have to be taken for reasons of
public security and order, public health or morality shall not be deemed "treatment less
favorable".

(3)(cid:9)
Such treatiment shall not relate to priviliges which either Contracting State
accords to investors of third States on account of its membership of, or association with, a
customs or economic union, a common market o- a free trade area or by virtue of a
double taxation agreement or other agreerneits regarding matters of taxation.
(4)(cid:9)
The provisions of this Article do not obl:ge a Contracting State to extend to
investors resident in the territory of the other Contracting State tax privileges, tax
exemptions and tax reductions which according to its tax laws are granted only to
investors resident in its territory.
(5)(cid:9)
The Confracting States shall within the frunework of their national legislation
give sympathetic consideration to applications for the entry and sojourn of persons of
either Contracting State who wish to enter the territory of the other Contracting State, in
connection with an investment; the same shall ipply to employed persons of either
Contracting State who in connection with an investment wish to enter the territory of the
other Contracting State and sojourn there to take ID employment. Applications for ‘7, ork
permits shall also be given sympathetic consideraticn.
(6)(cid:9)
The investors of either Contracting State art free to choose international means of
transport of persons or capital goods connected with an investment within the meaning of
this Agreement without prejudice to relevant bilateral or international agreements binding
on either Contracting State.

Article 4
Compensation in Case of Expropriation
Investments by investors of either Contracting State shall not be expropriated;
nationalized or Subjected to any other measure the ;ffects of which would be tantamount
to expropriation or nationalization in the territory )f the other Contracting State except
for the public benefit and against compensation. Sich compensation shall be equivalent
to the value of the expropriated investment immeliately before the date on which the
actual or threatened expropriation, nationalization or comparable measure has become
publicly known. The compensation shall be paid rkithout delay and shall carry the usual
bank interest until the time of payment; it shall be effectively realizable and freely
transferable. Provision shall have been made in an appropriate manner at or prior to the
time of expropriation, nationalization or comparabe measure for the determination and
payment of such compensation. The legality of any such expropriation, nationalization ,or
comparable measure and the amount of compensaton shall be subject to review by due
process of law.
Investors of either Contracting State whcse investments suffer losses in the
territory of the other Contracting State owing to wa• or other armed conflict, revolution, a
state of national emergency, or revolt, shall be accorded treatment no less favorable by
such other Contracting State than that which the tatter Contracting State accords te its
own investors as regards restitution, indemnification, compensation or other valuable
consideration. Such payments shall be freely transfirable.
Investors of either Contracting State shall eljoy most-favored-nation treatment in
the territory of the other Contracting State in respict of the matters provided for in this
Article.

Article 5
Free Transfe•
(1)(cid:9)
Each Contracting State shall guarantee all traisfers relating to an investment to be
made freely and without delay into and out of its terrtory. Such transfers include:
the principal and additional amounts ti maintain or increase the
Investment;
the returns;
the repaymem of loans;
the proceeds from the liquidation or tb sale of the whole or any part of the
investment;
the compensation provided for in Artide 4.
(2)(cid:9)
Transfers under this Article, Article 4 (1)
OT (2) or Article 6 shall be made at the
applicable rate of exchange on the day of transfer.
(3)(cid:9)
Should there be no foreign exchange markct the cross rate obtained from those
rates which would be applied by the International konetary Fund on the date of payment
for conversions of the currencies concerned into Special Drawing Rights shall apply.
(4)(cid:9)
Subject to the provision of Article 5 (1) a tansfer shall be deemed to have been
made "without delay" within the meaning of this Miele if effected within such period as
is normally required for the completion of transfer formalities. The said period shall
commence on the day on which the relevant reque:t has been submitted and may on no
account exceed tWo months.
(5)(cid:9)
Notwithstanding the foregoing paragraphs, a Contracting State may prevent a
transfer through the equitable, non-discriminatory tnd good faith application of its laws
relating to:
bankruptcy, insolvency or the protectioa of the rights of creditors;
issuing, trading or dealing in securities;

Article 6
Subrogation
If either Contracting State makes a payment to any of its investors under its scheme of
guarantees it has assumed in respect of an inves.ment in the territory of the other
Contracting State, the latter Contracting State shall, vithout prejudice to the rights of the
former Contractifig State under Article 9, recognize he assignment, whether under a law
-
Or pursuant to a legal transaction, of any right or claim of such investor to the former -
Contracting State.
Article 7
Application of other kules
(1)(cid:9)
If the legislation of either Contracting State Cr obligations under international law
existing at present or established hereafter between :he Contracting States in addition to
this Agreement contain a regulation, whether genera or specific, entitling investments by
investors of the other Contracting State to a treatmert more favorable than is provided for
by this Agreement, such regulation shall to the extnt that it is more favorable prevail
over this Agreement.
Each Contracting State shall observe any o:her obligation it has assumed with
regard to investments in its territory by investors if the other Contracting State, with
disputes arising from such obligations being redres;ed under the terms of the contriacts
underlying the obligations in accordance with Articit 10 (5).
With regard to the treattnent of income an
assets for the purpose of taxation,
precedence shall be given to the application of tie agreements in force at the time
between the Federal Republic of Germany and the islamic Republic of Pakistan for the
avoidance of double taxation in the field of taxes on ncome and assets.

Article 8
Scope of Applicat on
This Agreement shall also apply to investments trade prior to its entry into force by
investors of either Contracting State • in the territry of the other Contracting Slate
consistent with the latter's legislation. However, it :hall not apply to any dispute or any
claim concerning an investment which was already Inder judicial or arbitral process.
Article9
Settlement of Disputes between di( Contracting States
(1)(cid:9)
Any dispute between the Contracting Staes concerning the interpretation or
application of this Agreement should as far as possiole be settled by the governments of
the two Contracting States.
' If the dispute cannot thus be settled, it shall tpon the request of either Contracting
State be submitted to an arbitration tribunal.
Such arbitration tribunal shall be constituted ad hoc as follows: each Contracting
State shall appoint one member, and these two rnenbers shall agree upon a national of a
third State as their chairman to be appointed by the governments of the two Contracting
States. Such members shall be appointed within tw) months, and such chairman within
three months from the date on which either Contacting State has informed the other
Contracting State that it intends to submit the disputt to an arbitration tribunal.
(2)(cid:9)
If the periods specified in paragraph 3 thove have not been observed, either
Contracting State may, in the absence
Af any other arrangement, invite the
President of the International Court )f Justice to make the necessary
appointments. If the President is a natioral of either Contracting State or if he
is otherwise prevented from discharging he said function, the Vice-President

ng
State should make the necessary appointrnents.
(a)
The arbitration tribunal shall reach its decisions by a majority of votes.
Such decisions shall be binding. Eaci Contracting State shall bear the east
of its own member and of its represeitatives in the arbitration proceedi ngs.
The cost of the chairman and the renaming costs shall be borne in equal
parts by the Contracting States. The arbitration tribunal may male a
different regulation concerning cost,. In all other respects, the arbitration
tribunal shall determine its own procedure.
Article 10
Settlement of Disputes between a Contractirg State and an Investor of the other
Contracting State
(1)(cid:9)
Disputes arising from an investment between a Contracting State and an investor
of the other Contracting State which concern an alleged breach of an obligation of a
Contracting State under this Agreement should Es far as possible be settled amicably
between the parties in dispute.
If the dispute cannot be settled within six months of the date when it has
been raised in writing by one of he parties in dispute, it shall, al the
request of the investor be submitted to:
the competeat court in the Contracfng State in whose territory the
investment has been made; or
(b)(cid:9)
international arbitration under eithe::

Article 11
Relations between the Cotracting States
This Agreement shall be in force irrespective of wlether or not diplomatic or consular
relations exist between the Contracting States.
Article 12
Registration claise
R:egistration of this Agreement with the Secretariat ,4* the United Nations, in accordance
with Article 102 of the United Nations Charter, shall be initiated immediately following
its entry into force by the Contracting State in which the signing took place. The other
Contracting State shall be informed of registration, aid of the United Nations registration
number, as soon as this has been confirmed by the Se
.-Tetariat of the United Nations.
Article 13
Entry into Force, Duration aid Termination
This Agreement shall be subject to ratificaion; the instruments of ratification
shall be exchanged as soon as possible.
This Agreement shall enter into force one moth after the date of exchange of the
instruments of ratification. It shall remain in force fcr a period of ten years and shall he
extended thereafter for an unlimited period unless denounced in writing through
diplomatic channels by either Contracting State tvelve months before its expiration.
After the expiry of the period of ten years this Agreenent may be denounced at any time
by either Contracting State giving twelve months' notce.

In respect of investments made prior to the dae of termination of this Agreement,
the provisions of the preceding Articles shall continte to be effective for a further period
of fifteen years from the date of termination of this.Ageement.
Upon entry into force of this Agreement. the Treaty of 25 November 1(69
between Pakistan and the Federal Republic of Germiny for the Promotion and Protection
of Investments shall be terminated.
